commit.message:
Contributing docs updater Copilot skill (#11401)

# Description

New Copilot agent skill for maintaining contributor documentation
(`docs/contributing/`, `docs/architecture/`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`). The
skill provides structured workflows for creating, rewriting, reviewing,
and gap-analyzing contributor docs, plus a code review doc impact
assessment mode.

### Added
- `.github/skills/contributing-docs-updater/SKILL.md` — skill definition
with five modes: rewrite, create, gap analysis, doc review, and code
review impact assessment. Uses dynamic directory discovery instead of
hardcoded paths.
-
`.github/skills/contributing-docs-updater/references/writing-guidelines.md`
— writing standards reference covering two content types (Guide and
Reference), formatting, tone, cross-reference rules, and content
accuracy checks.

### Changed
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — registered the new skill in the
skills index.
- `.github/instructions/code-review.instructions.md` — added Step 4
(Assess Contributor Documentation Impact) to the code review checklist,
linking to the new skill.

## Type of change

- This pull request is a minor refactor, code cleanup, test improvement,
or other maintenance task and doesn't change the functionality of Radius
(issue link optional).

## Contributor checklist
Please verify that the PR meets the following requirements, where
applicable:

- An overview of proposed schema changes is included in a linked GitHub
issue.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio schema -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio schema -->
- A design document PR is created in the [design-notes
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/design-notes/), if new
APIs are being introduced.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio design-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio design-pr -->
- The design document has been reviewed and approved by Radius
maintainers/approvers.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio design-review -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio design-review -->
- A PR for the [samples
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/samples) is created, if
existing samples are affected by the changes in this PR.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio samples-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio samples-pr -->
- A PR for the [documentation
repository](https://github.com/radius-project/docs) is created, if the
changes in this PR affect the documentation or any user facing updates
are made.
    - [ ] Yes <!-- TaskRadio docs-pr -->
    - [x] Not applicable <!-- TaskRadio docs-pr -->

commit.message:
Harden non-coding agent guardrails with command allowlist and guard workflow (#178)

* Harden non-coding agent guardrails with command allowlist and guard workflow

Problem: The project agent violated its guardrails on 2026-03-09 by editing
code via the execute tool, pushing commits, and closing issues as fixed when
code was never merged to main (PR #174, issues #168/#175).

Root cause: The denylist approach (NEVER run git commit) was insufficient.
The agent rationalized around prohibitions when it found bugs to fix.

Three-layer defense:

1. Command ALLOWLIST (project.agent.md, product.agent.md):
   - Exhaustive list of permitted commands (gh issue, curl, cat/grep/jq)
   - Everything else FORBIDDEN (git add/commit/push, sed, gh pr create)
   - CHECKPOINT reminders at every phase transition
   - Identity anchoring: 'You are an ANALYST, not an engineer'
   - Issue-close verification: must confirm fix exists in main branch

2. Guard workflow (guard-agent-prs.yml):
   - Triggers on pull_request opened/reopened
   - Auto-closes PRs from non-coding agent branch patterns
   - Hard platform-level boundary that can't be bypassed by prompts

3. Documentation updates:
   - PROJECT-AGENT.md: describes allowlist and guard workflow
   - ARCHITECTURE.md: updated agent isolation section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix guard workflow: use PR author instead of actor, fix comment indentation

- Use github.event.pull_request.user.login instead of github.actor so
  the guard catches reopened PRs regardless of who reopened them
- Use heredoc for PR comment to avoid leading whitespace rendering as
  markdown code block
- Update PROJECT-AGENT.md to reflect the actual check

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Soften guard workflow docs — scope guarantee to matching branch patterns

* Add git show to product agent allowlist for consistency with docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix docs: clarify execute tool restriction is allowlist + guard, not absence of edit

commit.message:
Add compact mode to Poseidon circuit to reduce R1CS non-zeros (#471)

Adds set_compact() on PoseidonCircuit2 and SpongeCircuit. When enabled,
non-S-boxed elements are materialized each partial round, capping LC
term growth at ~2 terms instead of ~58 (for arity-2, 55 partial rounds).

Default is off, preserving existing behavior and digests for Nova's
internal augmented circuit.

commit.message:
fix: add PATCH fallback for close_task when CloseTask action unavailable

When the CRM environment lacks the CloseTask action and the bound
Close endpoint, fall back to a direct PATCH that sets both statecode
and statuscode on the task record. Fixes task close failures in
environments where neither action-based endpoint is registered.

Three-tier fallback chain:
1. CloseTask unbound action (primary)
2. tasks(id)/Microsoft.Dynamics.CRM.Close (bound action)
3. PATCH tasks(id) with statecode + statuscode (new)
